The first day of Muharram marks the beginning of the Islamic New Year. A time for reflection and gratitude.
The 10th of Muharram. A day of fasting and remembrance, commemorating the salvation of Prophet Musa (AS).
The Night Journey and Ascension of Prophet Muhammad (PBUH) from Makkah to Jerusalem and to the heavens.
The beginning of the blessed month of fasting, prayer, and spiritual reflection.
The Night of Decree, better than a thousand months. Seek it in the last ten nights of Ramadan.
The Festival of Breaking the Fast. A joyous celebration marking the end of Ramadan.
The 9th of Dhu al-Hijjah. Standing at Arafah is the essence of Hajj. Fasting on this day expiates sins.
The Festival of Sacrifice. Commemorating Prophet Ibrahim's (AS) willingness to sacrifice his son.
Location permission is required for Qibla direction
